title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opens to Franchise-Best $53 Million
text: A Quiet Place: Day One dominated its opening weekend with a domestic total of $53 million, marking the strongest debut in the franchise's history. The prequel surpassed both the original 2018 film ($50 million) and the 2021 sequel ($48 million). The horror thriller, starring Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn, added $45.5 million internationally for a global opening of $98.5 million. Directed by Michael Sarnoski, the film is set during the early stages of an alien invasion in New York City. Despite strong audience reception the film earned an 84% on Rotten Tomatoes and a B+ CinemaScore it finished second to Disney-Pixar's Inside Out 2, which continues its box office dominance with $57 million in its third weekend. According to Paramount's Chris Aronson, the film's success came from offering audiences a new city as the setting and fresh characters while maintaining the franchise's core appeal. The $67 million production positions well for its theatrical run, particularly given the positive critical reception.
